title: Santorini 2-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: Santorini 2-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 2-day trip to Santorini, Greece: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Akrotiri Archaeological Site and Museum of Prehistoric Thera. Prices from $1163 per person (estimate).
highlights: Private catamaran caldera cruise with sunset dinner
Sunset dinner overlooking the caldera in Oia
overview: Spend 2 days discovering Santorini, Greece — Mediterranean island climate with mild, breezy winters (highs 15°C) and hot, dry summers (highs 29°C); strong winds (the "meltemi") are common in summer. Late April to June and September to early October give warm weather, calm seas and lighter crowds than the packed July-August peak. This itinerary blends Akrotiri Archaeological Site, Museum of Prehistoric Thera, Oia sunset viewpoint with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
1 night accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Akrotiri Archaeological Site, Museum of Prehistoric Thera, Oia sunset viewpoint
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Santorini
itinerary.description: Land at Santorini (Thira) National Airport (JTR) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. With limited time on the ground, head straight out to Akrotiri Archaeological Site — Preserved Bronze Age Minoan settlement. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Santorini Highlights & Departure
itinerary.description: Start early with a visit to Museum of Prehistoric Thera — Artifacts from the Akrotiri excavation. Grab a last taste of local life before your private transfer to JTR for your onward flight.

travelTips: Book caldera-view accommodation in Oia or Imerovigli months ahead for peak season.
Arrive at the Oia sunset viewpoint at least an hour early in summer to get a spot.
Ferries between islands can be cancelled due to wind — build buffer days into island-hopping itineraries.
Red and black sand beaches get scorching hot midday — bring sandals for the walk to the water.
Renting an ATV is cheaper and more practical than taxis for reaching multiple villages in a day.
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Comfortable, grippy shoes for steep cobbled caldera steps
Sun protection — hat, high-SPF sunscreen, sunglasses
A light wind layer for breezy clifftop evenings
Swimwear plus a cover-up for beach-to-town transitions
thingsToAvoid: Overpaying at restaurants directly on the Oia sunset strip — quality doesn't always match the price.
Skipping travel insurance — ferry and flight delays due to weather are common.
Wandering onto caldera-view hotel terraces for photos — these are private property.
Driving rental ATVs without a valid international license — checks do happen.
Assuming all beaches are sandy — several are pebble or volcanic rock, uncomfortable barefoot.
shoppingAreas: Oia art galleries and jewelry shops
familyActivities: Boat trip to volcanic hot springs
luxuryExperiences: Private catamaran caldera cruise with sunset dinner
Private wine-pairing dinner at a caldera-view winery
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset dinner overlooking the caldera in Oia
Candlelit dinner on a cliffside terrace
budgetExperiences: Free sunset watching from Fira caldera walkway
Relax at Perissa black sand beach
Explore quiet Megalochori village for free
localEtiquette: Modest dress is expected when entering Orthodox churches — cover shoulders and knees.
Sunset viewing spots in Oia get extremely crowded; arrive early and be patient with space.
Tipping around 5-10% in restaurants is appreciated though not mandatory.
Many businesses close for a midday rest in shoulder season; plan around siesta hours.
Respect private property — many caldera-view spots are on hotel land, not public paths.
localTransportGuide: Renting an ATV or small car is the most flexible way to explore beyond Fira and Oia, since the island is hilly and bus routes are limited. Local KTEL buses run between main villages, and taxis are scarce and should be booked ahead for airport or ferry transfers.
bestTimeToVisit: Late April to June and September to early October give warm weather, calm seas and lighter crowds than the packed July-August peak.
weather: Mediterranean island climate with mild, breezy winters (highs 15°C) and hot, dry summers (highs 29°C); strong winds (the "meltemi") are common in summer.
visaInformation: Schengen area — visa-free entry for US, UK, Canadian, Australian and EU passport holders for stays up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Other nationalities should check Schengen short-stay visa requirements in advance.
averageFlightTime: ~9.8h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Santorini?
faqs.answer: Schengen area — visa-free entry for US, UK, Canadian, Australian and EU passport holders for stays up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Other nationalities should check Schengen short-stay visa requirements in advance.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Santorini?
faqs.answer: Late April to June and September to early October give warm weather, calm seas and lighter crowds than the packed July-August peak. Mediterranean island climate with mild, breezy winters (highs 15°C) and hot, dry summers (highs 29°C); strong winds (the "meltemi") are common in summer.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Santorini safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Santorini is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Santorini?
faqs.answer: The local currency is EUR. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: Yes — Santorini is one of our most-booked honeymoon destinations, especially the Sunset dinner overlooking the caldera in Oia experience.

reviews.author: Arjun Kowalski
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Volcano & Hot Springs boat tour in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Yuki van Dijk
reviews.title: Loved every moment
reviews.body: Museum of Prehistoric Thera alone was worth the trip, but combined with Perissa black sand beach this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Arjun Kowalski
reviews.title: Loved every moment
reviews.body: Booked this as a family of 4 trip and it was well paced. Fira to Oia coastal hike in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Chen Botha
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: Booked this as a group trip and it was well paced. ATV/quad bike rental day tour in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Priya Fernandez
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Perissa black sand beach in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Mohammed Silva
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: Imerovigli village alone was worth the trip, but combined with Perissa black sand beach this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Arjun Brown
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: Booked this as a family of 4 trip and it was well paced. Santo Wines winery tour & tasting in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Elena Andersen
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Imerovigli village and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Nadia Costa
reviews.title: Great value for money
reviews.body: Santorini is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Perissa black sand beach. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Lucas Larsen
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to ATV/quad bike rental day tour and Santorini Arts Factory. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Mohammed Rossi
reviews.title: Consultant made it effortless
reviews.body: Fira caldera-edge walkway alone was worth the trip, but combined with Fira caldera-edge walkway this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.
